prompt:
Fix NVIDIA modesetting on classic-laddie so the GPU drives display from boot instead of leaving Hyprland stuck on the EFI simple-framebuffer at 1024×768.

## Context — what happened on classic-laddie this morning

After last night's rebuild (Generation 261, NixOS 26.05.20260427.1c3fe55, NVIDIA driver 595.58.03), the system booted but the desktop came up at 1024×768. Investigation showed:

- `hardware.nvidia.modesetting.enable = true` is already set in `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix:33`.
- Kernel cmdline includes `nvidia-drm.modeset=1 nvidia-drm.fbdev=1` (good — these are correctly emitted by the modesetting option).
- BUT `lsmod` showed only `nvidia` and `nvidia_uvm` loaded; `nvidia_drm` and `nvidia_modeset` were missing.
- `/dev/dri/` only had `card0` (the EFI simple-framebuffer); no NVIDIA DRM card existed.
- `/sys/module/nvidia_drm` did not exist at all — nothing tried to load nvidia_drm during boot.
- `nvidia-drm.ko` was present at `/run/booted-system/kernel-modules/lib/modules/6.19.12-zen1/misc/nvidia-drm.ko` — module built fine, just wasn't loaded.

After manually `sudo modprobe nvidia_drm`, the modules loaded correctly, `/dev/dri/` gained `card1` + `renderD128`, and (after a display-manager restart) the desktop came up at native resolution. So the modules work; they're just not being autoloaded at boot.

This is a regression somewhere — possibly from the NVIDIA 595.x driver bump, possibly from a kernel/initrd change, possibly from how the Zen kernel + modesetting option interact. Either way, the explicit fix is to ensure these modules load early.

## Change to make

Edit `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix`. Inside the existing config (alongside or just after the `hardware.nvidia` block at line 32+), add:

```nix
# Force-load NVIDIA display modules in initrd so the GPU drives the
# framebuffer from boot. Without this, hardware.nvidia.modesetting.enable
# wasn't enough on driver 595.58.03 + linux-zen 6.19.12 — the kernel
# command line set nvidia-drm.modeset=1 but nothing actually loaded
# nvidia_drm, leaving Hyprland stuck on EFI fb at 1024x768.
boot.initrd.kernelModules = [
  "nvidia"
  "nvidia_modeset"
  "nvidia_uvm"
  "nvidia_drm"
];
```

If `boot.initrd.kernelModules` is already set elsewhere in this file or an imported module, *append* to it rather than overwriting — use `++` or merge appropriately. Read the whole file first to understand the existing structure.

## Investigation step before editing
- Read `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` in full.
- `grep -rn 'initrd.kernelModules\|kernelModules' modules/ hosts/classic-laddie/` to see if these modules are set anywhere else.
- If a common module already sets `boot.kernelModules` (post-boot, not initrd) for nvidia, decide whether to add to that or use the initrd variant. The initrd variant is safer for display because it loads before the EFI framebuffer gets locked.

## CRITICAL constraints
- **No local nix tooling whatsoever.** No `nixos-rebuild`, no `nix build`, no `nix flake check`, no `nix eval`. Cosmo CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) does dry-run builds on a runner; that's the validation gate.
- The user's machine just suffered through 8+ hours of building from source last night and is *not* doing it again as part of testing this PR.
- Don't reformat unrelated lines.
- The change is host-specific (classic-laddie only), so don't put it in a common module.

## Acceptance criteria
- `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` has nvidia display modules added to `boot.initrd.kernelModules` (or equivalent existing list).
- PR description covers:
  - The symptom (1024×768 framebuffer after rebuild)
  - The root cause (nvidia_drm not autoloading despite `modesetting.enable = true`)
  - The investigation evidence (lsmod, /dev/dri, /sys/module observations)
  - Why initrd-level loading is the right fix
  - That the user has already confirmed manual modprobe works, so this just makes it persistent
- CI green (pipeline tracks this).
